U
Oracle Events
OE-R1: Search emails for Carmen Reyes' housing reply about Priya Suresh Denver options.
Tool: search_emails (default), query related to "Carmen" or "Priya Suresh" or "Denver housing" (or similar).
Discovery: Three options. Option A (1840 Blake St, fenced yard) has 50-lb dog cap. Option B (2200 Champa St) no yard. Option C (3100 Zuni St, LoHi) any size dog, $2,650/month vs $2,100 budget.
OE-R2: Search emails for Priya Suresh's direct email about ESA documentation.
Tool: search_emails (default), query related to "Priya" or "ESA" or "Atlas" (or similar).
Discovery: Priya has ESA letter from therapist on file with HR. Believes Fair Housing Act accommodations override weight restrictions.
OE-R3: Search emails for Greg Pallone's moving quote for Priya Suresh.
Tool: search_emails (default), query related to "Greg" or "Swift" or "moving quote" (or similar).
Discovery: $2,800 base. $450 specialty handling surcharge for soldering/rework station (requires anti-static ESD-safe packaging sourced from Seattle, adds 1 day to pickup), or Priya self-packs with no liability coverage.
OE-R4: Search emails for Michael Stern's flight confirmation for Priya.
Tool: search_emails (default), query related to "Michael" or "flight" or "United" (or similar).
Discovery: United UA 1547, April 14, PDX to DEN. United does not allow ESAs in-cabin, only psychiatric service dogs. Need checked pet baggage ($200 each way) or pet shipping service.
OE-R5: Search Slack #operations for Chloe's reassignment message.
Tool: conversations_search_messages (default), query related to "Terrence" or "Camille" or "Suki" (or similar).
Discovery: Chloe asking Javier to take over Terrence and Camille from Suki.
OE-R6: Search Slack #operations for Suki Patel's handoff notes.
Tool: conversations_search_messages (default), query related to "Suki" or "handoff" (or similar).
Discovery: Terrence has ADA housing gap (May 1 vs April 22). Camille's Seattle apartment pre-secured by Vectral (no housing sourcing needed). Boston pickup and flight booked with Swift, but movers/flight alignment with lease start date not yet confirmed.
OE-R7: Search emails for Carmen Reyes' ADA housing reply about Terrence Nakamura (sent to Suki, not Javier).
Tool: search_emails (default), query related to "Carmen Terrence" or "ADA San Francisco" or "Nakamura" (or similar).
Discovery: 450 Fell St $3,200/month available April 22. 1200 Van Ness Ave $2,800/month available May 1 only. 9-day gap.
OE-R8: Search Airtable for Priya Suresh relocation record.
Tool: airtable_search_records (non-default), base_id "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name "Relocations", field_name "Name", value "Priya Suresh".
Discovery: recPriyaSureshPortlandDenver. Assigned to Javier. ESA German Shepherd Atlas 85 lbs.
OE-R9: Search Airtable for Terrence Nakamura relocation record.
Tool: airtable_search_records (non-default), base_id "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name "Relocations", field_name "Name", value "Terrence Nakamura".
Discovery: recTerrenceNakamuraAustinSF. Assigned to Suki Patel. ADA wheelchair user.
OE-R10: Search Airtable for Camille Oduya relocation record.
Tool: airtable_search_records (non-default), base_id "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name "Relocations", field_name "Name", value "Camille Oduya".
Discovery: recCamilleOduyaBostonSeattle. Assigned to Suki Patel. Apartment PRE-SECURED by Vectral at 1422 Minor Ave Apt 7B. NO sourcing needed. $3,500 enhanced stipend.
OE-R11: Search Slack #customer-engagement for Mina's message about Rachel's board update.
Tool: conversations_search_messages (default), query related to "Mina" or "Rachel" or "board" (or similar).
Discovery: Rachel needs status on all 5 Vectral moves for board presentation. Mina pinging Javier for consolidated status.
OE-R12: Search emails for Chloe's formal handoff confirmation email.
Tool: search_emails (default), query related to "Chloe" or "coordinator reassignment" (or similar).
Discovery: Javier is coordinator of record. Must update Airtable, resolve housing, notify Mina.
OE-R13: Search emails for Chloe Vance's laptop/network issues email mentioning her personal email.
Tool: search_emails (default), query related to "Chloe" or "laptop" or "personal email" (or similar).
Discovery: Chloe's work laptop crashed. Requests CC to personal email chloe_shines@gmail.com for anything urgent until replacement arrives Monday.
OE-W1: Send email to carmen.reyes@urbannestsolutions.com with Priya's ESA/FHA accommodation request for 1840 Blake St.
Tool: send_email. Sender: javier.morales@moveops.com. Recipient: carmen.reyes@urbannestsolutions.com.
OE-W2: Same email to Carmen must also address Terrence Nakamura's ADA housing gap resolution.
Tool: send_email (same call as OE-W1 or separate).
OE-W3: Send email to greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com with decision on the $450 specialty handling surcharge (approve ESD packaging option or instruct Priya to self-pack with no liability coverage).
Tool: send_email. Sender: javier.morales@moveops.com. Recipient: greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com.

Tool: airtable_update_records. table_id: tblRelocations01.
OE-W5: Update Airtable record recCamilleOduyaBostonSeattle coordinator to Javier Morales.
Tool: airtable_update_records. table_id: tblRelocations01.
OE-W6: Send email to mina.hashimoto@moveops.com with consolidated board update summary.
Tool: send_email. Sender: javier.morales@moveops.com. Recipient: mina.hashimoto@moveops.com.
OE-W7: Post message in Slack #operations (C006) with detailed plan for Chloe.
Tool: conversations_add_message. Channel: C006.
OE-W8: Send email to chloe.vance@moveops.com with status update on Terrence and Camille handoff, CC chloe_shines@gmail.com (personal email due to laptop/network issues).
Tool: send_email. Sender: javier.morales@moveops.com. Recipient: chloe.vance@moveops.com. CC: chloe_shines@gmail.com.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

2 The email to [carmen.reyes@urbannestsolutions.com](mailto:carmen.reyes@urbannestsolutions.com) references the Fair Housing Act or FHA as the legal basis for Priya Suresh's ESA accommodation (or similar).
outcome
 ✓✓✓—✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The email body to Carmen explicitly references the 'Fair Housing Act (FHA)' and 'reasonable accommodation' multiple times as the legal basis for Priya's ESA.
✓Run #2The email body to carmen.reyes@urbannestsolutions.com explicitly states 'Under the Fair Housing Act, ESA documentation generally requires landlords to grant a reasonable accommodation' and further references 'FHA guidelines' — the legal framework is clearly communicated.
✓Run #3The email body to carmen.reyes@urbannestsolutions.com explicitly states: 'Under the Fair Housing Act, landlords are required to make reasonable accommodations for ESAs' and instructs her to submit 'a formal reasonable accommodation request'.
—Run #4No justification
✓Run #5The email body to carmen.reyes@urbannestsolutions.com explicitly references 'Under the Fair Housing Act (FHA), landlords are required to make reasonable accommodations for emotional support animals' and instructs a 'formal FHA reasonable accommodation request'.
✓Run #6The email to Carmen explicitly references 'the Fair Housing Act (specifically the FHA's reasonable accommodation provisions)' as the legal basis for waiving the weight cap for Priya's ESA.
3 The email to [carmen.reyes@urbannestsolutions.com](mailto:carmen.reyes@urbannestsolutions.com) states that the 50-lb weight cap does not apply to documented ESAs (or similar).
outcome
 ✓✓✓—✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The email body to Carmen states: 'The landlord's 50-lb dog weight cap does not apply to an ESA with proper documentation — the FHA requires landlords to grant a reasonable accommodation for ESAs regardless of pet policies.'
✓Run #2The email to Carmen explicitly states 'Under FHA guidelines, the 50-lb weight cap should not apply to a documented emotional support animal,' directly communicating that the restriction is overridden by ESA status.
✓Run #3The email body explicitly states: 'The 50-lb cap does not apply when a tenant has proper ESA documentation.'
—Run #4No justification
✓Run #5The email to Carmen states 'weight/breed restrictions generally do not apply to ESAs when the tenant provides legitimate documentation' and that the FHA reasonable accommodation request means 'the 50-lb weight cap should not apply to her emotional support animal'.
✓Run #6The Carmen email states: 'landlords are legally required to waive breed and weight restrictions for ESAs with proper documentation' and 'The landlord cannot legally deny Atlas based on the 50-lb weight cap if Priya provides her ESA letter.'
4 The email to [carmen.reyes@urbannestsolutions.com](mailto:carmen.reyes@urbannestsolutions.com) addresses Terrence Nakamura's ADA housing gap, referencing either the availability timing issue or a request for temporary ADA-accessible housing (or similar).
outcome
 ✓✓✓—✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The email to Carmen has a full section on 'TERRENCE NAKAMURA - SAN FRANCISCO ADA HOUSING (Gap Issue)' discussing Option A (April 22 availability) vs. Option B (May 1, 9-day gap) and requesting Carmen lock in 450 Fell St.
✓Run #2The email to Carmen includes a dedicated section on Terrence Nakamura's ADA situation, explicitly noting the 9-day gap with Option B (May 1 availability vs. April 22 move end date) and selecting Option A at 450 Fell St to eliminate the gap.
✓Run #3The email to Carmen directly addresses Terrence's ADA situation including 'The April 22 availability aligns with his move end date, which eliminates the 9-day gap that Option B would create.'
—Run #4No justification
✓Run #5The email to Carmen addresses Terrence's ADA situation, selecting Option A available April 22 to avoid the 9-day gap that Option B (May 1 availability) would create, and explicitly references the 9-day gap with $1,800–$2,200 in temp housing costs.
✓Run #6The Carmen email has an entire section on 'TERRENCE NAKAMURA — ADA HOUSING IN SAN FRANCISCO' addressing the 9-day gap between April 22 arrival and May 1 availability, asking Carmen to resend full details and offering to arrange interim accessible housing if needed.
5 An email was sent (via send_email) from [javier.morales@moveops.com](mailto:javier.morales@moveops.com) to [greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com](mailto:greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com).
outcome

✓Run #6The agent used reply_to_email (email_id 66af4e8b19de4e00817ea4a9c4b71bee) with sender javier.morales@moveops.com, replying to Greg's moving quote email. The reply was sent from Javier to Greg.
6 The email to [greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com](mailto:greg.pallone@swiftrelocations.com) approves the $450 specialty handling surcharge for Priya's soldering station (or similar).
outcome
 ✓✓✓—✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The reply_to_email to Greg explicitly states: 'I want to go with Option 1 — approve the $450 specialty handling surcharge for the soldering station. Total confirmed: $2,800 (standard household) + $450 (ESD-safe specialty handling for soldering station) = $3,250.' Self-pack option was not selected.
✓Run #2The reply to Greg (via reply_to_email) clearly states 'Let's go with Option 1 — approve the $450 specialty handling surcharge for the soldering station' with a confirmed total of $3,250. Self-pack was not selected. The content of the message meets this criterion regardless of the tool used.
